changelog: Mobile UI redesigned — Meloplayer-inspired bottom tab bar with glowing active indicator, floating mini-player with accent-colored play button, touch-optimized cards with :active scale feedback, 2-column search grid, larger tap targets (44px+) throughout, safe-area insets for notched phones
Browse tab replaced with Home tab — docs-style landing page with Glitchi-Stream title, version badge, full changelog, and editable DEV notes section (static/dev-notes.json)
Playlist download now auto-zips and delivers to device — batch playlist downloads collect all completed filenames into a ZIP and trigger browser download automatically

Web Worker polling — download progress runs in a dedicated thread (static/download-poll-worker.js), never throttled when browser tab is hidden; all setInterval/setTimeout replaced with worker-driven events
Playlist bugs fixed — openPlaylistView classList mismatch (close button now works), smarter local file matching (artist + track name word overlap), clear toast when tracks aren't downloaded yet
Search speed doubled — debounce reduced from 400ms to 250ms, client-side result cache with 60s TTL and 50-entry limit for instant repeat searches
Browse page loaded server-side — root route embedded all browse data as window.__BROWSE_DATA__ inline script so the page painted with zero API calls (removed when Browse became Home)

Playlist track count bug fixed — fetch_playlist_tracks now uses spotapi.playlist.PublicPlaylist.paginate_playlist() instead of the non-existent song.get_playlist() that was falling through to a random search query
Server-side 60s HTML cache on root route — prevented hammering SpotAPI on rapid refreshes (removed when Browse became Home)
8-second timeout on SpotAPI calls — page still loads if SpotAPI is slow or unreachable
18-theme system preserved across all pages with smooth CSS custom property transitions
Spotify track/album/artist detail preview with stream + download + add-to-playlist actions
All dead CSS purged — removed browse-specific .h-card, .h-scroll, .genre-chip, .browse-hero rules after Home tab migration
devNotes:
## Development Notes

Edit this section in `static/dev-notes.json` to add your own notes.

### Current Architecture
- **Backend**: FastAPI (app/main.py) with SpotAPI for Spotify metadata — no API keys required
- **Downloads**: yt-dlp / SpotDL via asyncio subprocess, task-based progress tracking with semaphore (max 3 concurrent)
- **Streaming**: yt-dlp subprocess → stdout → browser via httpx streaming response — nothing written to disk
- **Frontend**: Vanilla JS (static/app.js), no framework, no build step — static files served directly
- **Themes**: 18 CSS custom property themes in static/styles.css with data-theme attribute switching
- **Worker**: Web Worker (static/download-poll-worker.js) polls /download/progress every 1s in a separate thread

### Key Files
| File | Purpose |
|------|--------|
| app/main.py | FastAPI entry point, routers, static mount |
| app/api/browse.py | Browse/discover endpoints (categories, featured, new releases, trending, /browse/all) |
| app/api/download.py | Download task management, playlist batch |
| app/api/search.py | Spotify search proxy |
| app/api/playlists.py | ZIP creation, album tracks |
| app/api/stream.py | yt-dlp streaming proxy |
| app/services/downloader_service.py | SpotiFLAC/yt-dlp/SpotDL download logic, playlist track fetching |
| app/services/spotify_search_service.py | SpotAPI search, browse categories, album tracks |
| app/services/download_task_manager.py | In-memory task tracker with semaphore + TTL cleanup |
| static/app.js | Entire frontend — routing, search, downloads, playlists, streaming, themes |
| static/styles.css | All 18 theme definitions + responsive layout |
| static/index.html | SPA shell — sidebar, tabs, modals, mini-player, now-playing sheet |
| static/download-poll-worker.js | Web Worker for background-safe download progress polling |
| static/dev-notes.json | This file — version, changelog, editable DEV notes |

### Known Issues
- None reported

### Upcoming
- Queue system for sequential downloads (currently fires all at once, backend semaphore at 3)
- Equalizer in Now Playing sheet
- Offline PWA support with Service Worker
- Shuffle/repeat functionality in Now Playing
- Pull-to-refresh on mobile
